content:
One small realization completely changed the way I think about the future of programming.

6 min read
1 hour ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

A few days ago, I was sitting with a friend who had just started learning programming.
He looked genuinely worried.
“Should I stop learning to code?” he asked.
“AI writes code now.”
I smiled because I’ve been hearing the same question from my other friends too….
Open LinkedIn and you’ll find someone claiming software development is dead.
Scroll through YouTube and you’ll see thumbnails screaming that AI has replaced programmers.
Then you open X, and another developer proudly shares how AI built an entire application in ten minutes.
Honestly?
No wonder people are confused.
I’ve spent years writing software, learning new technologies, and watching this industry evolve faster than almost anyone expected. And after seeing what AI can actually do not what social media says it can do I don’t think developers are disappearing.
I think we’re witnessing one of the biggest shifts in software engineering history.
